title: VALAP protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dbnp2","text":"VALAP is a biologically inert method for sealing coverslips, making it usefulfor live cell imaging.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ak8m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"btu96","text":"Mix together:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9a7n8","text":"In a disposable but heat resistant container, combine equal parts by weight of the three components.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7r06v","text":"In a fume hood, heat the container at a low temperature to slowly melt ingredients together, mixing occasionally. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"38m82","text":"When it has combined into a homogenous liquid, carefully pour the liquid into small jars or bottles for storage.  Allow it to cool before placing the cap on.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"8eufl","text":"NEVER leave unattended on the hot plate! Valapleft unattended on a hot plate will vaporize into the room and will re-solidify on other surfaces.  This includes optics if there's a microscope in the room!","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":40}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c9qim","text":"Do not overheat!","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3m83l","text":"Vaseline (also called petrolatum)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e8n8j","text":"Lanolin","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ld40","text":"Paraffin wax (flakes work best)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7eq2","text":"Disposable container that can be heated on a hot plate","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1166v","text":"A disposable stirring device that can withstand heat (or glass rod that can be cleaned)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"47h0u","text":"The Vaseline, lanolin, and paraffin can be purchased from Fisher/VWR or, oftentimes, at your local drug store.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Put the jar or bottle on a hotplate on the lowest temperature setting. Do not leave unattended!!!
Wait for the Valap to melt fully.
Using a cotton-tipped applicator, make sure it is well loaded with liquid Valap.
Quickly wipe the Valap along the open edge of the coverslip, half on the coverslip and half on the slide.
Repeat for the rest of the coverslip edges that need to be sealed.
Be careful not to get Valap on microscope optics! It is very difficult to remove and it's presence will significantly degrade image quality.
This method uses a small metal spatula and an alcohol burner.
Use the spatula to take a small amount of valap from the container.
Hold it over the flame to melt it.
Put a small bead of valap along the open edge of the coverslip.
Repeat for the other sides of the coverslip Both methods take a bit of practice to do quickly. Method 2 creates a smaller profile and does not encroach on the coverslip surface as much as Method 1.
creator: Eadewunm
